evals.prompt: add search functionality to the user list page
evals.expected_output: Classifies as feat-new, runs /grill-me, /to-prd, /gsd-progress --next in sequence. No user questions.

assertions.name: feat-new classification
assertions.description: Intent is classified as feat-new (not fix or feat-continue)

assertions.name: grill-me invoked first
assertions.description: First sub-skill in sequence is /grill-me (not /grill-with-docs)

assertions.name: full pipeline announced
assertions.description: All three steps announced: /grill-me → /to-prd → /gsd-progress --next

assertions.name: no user questions
assertions.description: No questions asked to user (DESCRIPTION was clear)

assertions.name: no --ui pipeline
assertions.description: Does NOT invoke /gsd-ui-phase or /frontend-design (--ui flag absent)

evals.expected_output: Classifies as feat-continue, runs /gsd-progress --next only. No grill or prd.

assertions.name: feat-continue classification
assertions.description: Intent is classified as feat-continue

assertions.name: gsd-progress --next only
assertions.description: Only /gsd-progress --next is invoked — no /grill-me, no /to-prd

assertions.name: no user questions
assertions.description: No questions asked to user

evals.prompt: the submit button on the checkout form doesn't respond when clicked
evals.expected_output: Classifies as fix (no Jira). Claude reads code directly, no root-cause question to user, /diagnose not immediately invoked.

assertions.name: fix classification
assertions.description: Intent is classified as fix (not feat-new)

assertions.name: no Jira pipeline
assertions.description: Does NOT invoke /fix-jira (no Jira URL present)

assertions.name: no root-cause question
assertions.description: Does NOT ask user whether root cause is clear — Claude takes responsibility

assertions.name: read-code first action
assertions.description: First action is reading relevant code, not invoking /diagnose immediately

evals.expected_output: Classifies as fix-jira, runs /fix-jira with URL as argument.

assertions.name: Jira URL detected
assertions.description: Jira URL pattern (atlassian.net/browse/...) is correctly detected

assertions.name: fix-jira pipeline
assertions.description: Routes to /fix-jira (not direct fix attempt)

assertions.name: URL passed as argument
assertions.description: /fix-jira is called with the Jira URL as argument

assertions.name: no direct fix attempt
assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to fix directly — delegates to /fix-jira

evals.prompt: --ui build a landing page for our new product launch
evals.expected_output: Detects --ui flag, checks for ROADMAP.md to branch between GSD project path and quick prototype path. Both paths include /frontend-design and awesome-design-md. Deliver includes L3 browser verification via /webapp-testing before opening PR.

assertions.name: --ui flag detected
assertions.description: UI_MODE=true is set from the --ui flag

assertions.name: ROADMAP.md check performed
assertions.description: Skill explicitly checks whether .planning/ROADMAP.md exists to branch between GSD project path and quick prototype path

assertions.name: correct path for environment
assertions.description: If ROADMAP.md present → GSD path (grill-me → to-prd → gsd-ui-phase → gsd-progress); if absent → prototype path (grill-me → awesome-design-md → frontend-design). Either is correct given the environment.

assertions.name: frontend-design invoked on executed path
assertions.description: /frontend-design appears in the pipeline for whichever path was taken (prototype path: listed as sub-skill 3; GSD path: included in execution phases)

assertions.name: L3 browser verification in Deliver
assertions.description: Deliver phase includes browser/UI verification step (/webapp-testing or local golden path walkthrough) before opening the PR

evals.prompt: --merge fix the null pointer crash in the payment processor
evals.expected_output: Detects --merge flag → MERGE_MODE=true. Classifies as fix (no Jira). After fix is implemented and Deliver runs: merge the PR, then checkout main and pull before ending — branch is treated as spent after squash merge.

assertions.name: --merge flag detected
assertions.description: MERGE_MODE=true is set from the --merge flag

assertions.name: fix classification
assertions.description: Remaining description classified as fix (not feat-new)

assertions.name: deliver merge step mentioned
assertions.description: Skill mentions it will merge the PR once review loop is clean (not just stop at PR)

assertions.name: post-merge branch hygiene mentioned
assertions.description: Skill mentions switching to main (git checkout main / git pull) after merge, not reusing the feature branch

evals.prompt: --ui redesign the settings page to add a notification preferences section
evals.expected_output: Detects --ui flag. Deliver phase includes L3 browser verification step — either delegates to /webapp-testing or notes local app walkthrough before opening the PR. This is in addition to L1 tests and L2 PRD acceptance.

assertions.name: --ui flag detected
assertions.description: UI_MODE=true is set from the --ui flag

assertions.name: L3 browser verification mentioned
assertions.description: Deliver section references browser/UI verification step (webapp-testing, golden path, or local app walkthrough) before opening PR

assertions.name: webapp-testing delegation mentioned
assertions.description: Skill mentions /webapp-testing as the delegate for browser verification (when available)

assertions.name: browser step before PR
assertions.description: The browser verification is positioned before opening the PR, not after

evals.prompt: We're partway through the `coding` skill in `--ui` mode. The frontend change — a new notification-preferences section on the settings page — is now fully implemented. I just ran `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npm run build` (succeeded, zero errors). That all passed, so I think we're good to ship: go ahead and open the PR now.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT open the PR on compile-green alone. In UI_MODE, tsc+build are necessary-but-insufficient because neither renders a pixel or fires a click; the skill must run the L3 browser walkthrough (delegate /webapp-testing or launch locally) of the golden path BEFORE the PR, and push back on 'compile passed = ready to ship'. This eval reproduces the GO-2611 deviation at the exact Deliver decision point — it tests execution-time adherence, not just plan-time recall.

assertions.name: refuses-compile-as-ship-ticket
assertions.description: Does NOT open the PR on the strength of tsc + build alone; treats a green compile/build as necessary-but-insufficient in UI_MODE

assertions.name: requires-browser-walkthrough-before-pr
assertions.description: Insists on an L3 real-browser walkthrough of the golden path before any PR is opened

assertions.name: names-webapp-testing-delegate
assertions.description: Names /webapp-testing as the browser-verification delegate (or falls back to launching locally — not skipping)

assertions.name: pushes-back-on-compile-equals-done
assertions.description: Explicitly explains why compile/build is not enough rather than silently complying with 'open the PR now'

evals.prompt: add search functionality to the user list page
evals.expected_output: feat-new routed to /grill-me → /to-prd → /gsd-progress --next. Sub-skills are invoked DIRECTLY (EAFP): no SKILL.md existence pre-check, no 'if X is installed' hedge. Setup is mentioned only as an error-recovery fallback, never as an upfront gate.

assertions.name: feat-new classification
assertions.description: Intent is classified as feat-new (use grill-me)

assertions.name: direct-invocation-no-precheck
assertions.description: Does NOT verify/stat a sub-skill SKILL.md or run a detection/installed-check step before invoking the first sub-skill — it invokes directly

assertions.name: no-if-installed-hedge
assertions.description: Does NOT hedge any step with conditional existence language ('if grill-me is installed', 'if the skill exists, otherwise...')

assertions.name: setup-only-as-error-recovery
assertions.description: Setup/install of sub-skills, if mentioned, is framed strictly as a fallback triggered only when an invocation errors — not an upfront checklist

evals.prompt: --codex continue phase 3 (the project already has a .planning/ROADMAP.md, so this is GSD-backed work)
evals.expected_output: feat-continue under CODEX_MODE on a GSD project. Codex is delegated to DIRECTLY (EAFP): no upfront 'confirm Codex is ready' readiness gate before the first delegated step. Codex prerequisites (login + workflow.* flags) appear only as the fallback to run IF a delegated step errors. The plan-phase→plan-review-convergence and execute-phase --cross-ai swaps are still preserved.

assertions.name: codex-mode detected
assertions.description: Recognizes --codex / CODEX_MODE and that .planning/ROADMAP.md makes --codex applicable

assertions.name: direct-delegation-no-readiness-gate
assertions.description: Does NOT insert a blocking Codex-readiness pre-check before the first delegated step — proceeds to delegate directly

assertions.name: codex-setup-only-on-error
assertions.description: Codex prerequisites (codex login, workflow.cross_ai_command, workflow.plan_review_convergence) are mentioned only as the fallback IF a delegated step errors, not as an upfront confirmation

assertions.name: swap-semantics-preserved
assertions.description: Still applies the swaps: plan-phase → /gsd-plan-review-convergence --codex, execute-phase gains --cross-ai; other gsd commands untouched

evals.prompt: https://acme.atlassian.net/browse/PERF-42 — intermittent 504s on the report-export endpoint under load. [Eval setup: you classified this as fix-jira, created scoped branch fix/PERF-42 on a clean tree, invoked /fix-jira. It returned. The status file at .coding/fix-jira/PERF-42.status.json reads: {"schema_version":1,"issue_key":"PERF-42","branch":"fix/PERF-42","head_sha":"<matches branch HEAD>","run_started":true,"fix_implemented":false,"attempted_fix_left_bug_unresolved":true,"outcome":"blocked","blocker":"Reproduced the failing load test, but the root cause oscillates between connection-pool exhaustion and a cache-stampede path; 5 honest debugging attempts, no stable fix."}] What do you do next?
evals.expected_output: Routes on the two booleans: fix_implemented=false + attempted_fix_left_bug_unresolved=true is the TDD-stuck signal => escalate to /diagnose, handing it only status_path + branch + issue_key + head_sha (NOT pre-parsed detail fields). Does NOT read/route on `outcome`. Does NOT enter Deliver/open PR. Does NOT write a JIRA resolution. Only surfaces to human if /diagnose also fails.

assertions.name: routes-on-attempted-fix-flag
assertions.description: Triggers escalation off attempted_fix_left_bug_unresolved=true (the stuck-on-bug signal), NOT off the `outcome` value — does not switch on outcome/payload

assertions.name: dispatches-diagnose
assertions.description: Escalates to /diagnose rather than surfacing-and-stopping

assertions.name: diagnose-context-no-field-coupling
assertions.description: Hands /diagnose only status_path + branch + issue_key + head_sha; does not pre-parse fix-jira detail fields (failing_test/blocker/payload)

assertions.name: no-deliver-no-premature-jira
assertions.description: Does NOT enter Deliver/open a PR and does NOT write a JIRA resolution; any JIRA write waits for a real outcome

evals.prompt: https://acme.atlassian.net/browse/AUTH-9 — login returns 500 for SSO users. [Eval setup: you classified this as fix-jira, created a branch, invoked /fix-jira passing status_path .coding/fix-jira/AUTH-9.status.json. It returned this status file: {"schema_version":1,"issue_key":"AUTH-9","branch":"main","head_sha":"<HEAD>","run_started":false,"fix_implemented":false,"attempted_fix_left_bug_unresolved":false,"note":"Refused to edit: landed on protected branch 'main', not a scoped fix branch — aborted before any work."}] What do you do next?
evals.expected_output: run_started=false means /fix-jira never started — a handoff/setup error the orchestrator owns (it handed a protected/wrong branch). coding surfaces it as a setup error and fixes its own handoff (e.g. create the correct scoped branch); it does NOT escalate to /diagnose (nothing to diagnose — the bug was never attempted) and does NOT enter Deliver. attempted_fix_left_bug_unresolved=false confirms it is not the stuck-on-bug path.

assertions.name: recognizes-handoff-error
assertions.description: Reads run_started=false as a setup/handoff error the orchestrator owns (bad branch handed to /fix-jira), not a bug outcome

assertions.name: does-not-diagnose
assertions.description: Does NOT escalate to /diagnose — a preflight/handoff abort is not a 'genuinely stuck on the bug' condition

assertions.name: no-deliver
assertions.description: Does NOT enter Deliver / open a PR (no fix exists)

assertions.name: surfaces-or-repairs-setup
assertions.description: Surfaces the handoff error and/or repairs its own setup (correct scoped branch) rather than treating it as a terminal bug result or blaming the fixer
